card.intuition: A way to encapsulate a 'private' DOM tree inside an element, completely isolated from the main document's CSS and JavaScript.
card.analogy: A tinted car window: from the outside (main DOM), you just see a car. Inside (Shadow DOM), there is a whole dashboard, seats, and people that are hidden from public view and don't get 'wet' if it rains outside (CSS leaked).
when_to_use: Creating widgets or libraries (like a Video Player) that shouldn't be affected by the host site's styles
gotchas: Shadow DOM is NOT for security; it is for isolation. You can still reach it with JS if you have the reference
Legacy global CSS (like a reset.css) won't style elements inside a Shadow Root unless you explicitly use CSS variables (--var) or ::part

exercise.prompt: Identify the method used to attach a shadow root to an element.
